company.name: Cellanome, Inc.
company.mission: To build a foundational platform that connects the dynamic behavior of living cells to their underlying molecular identities — at the scale and resolution needed to transform drug discovery, cell therapy, and basic research.
company.one_liner: A foundational platform for live-cell biology — bridging the gap between what cells do and what they express, at unprecedented scale.

sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.subtitle: The Missing Link in Cell Biology
sections.content:
Cellanome is a Foster City, California-based life sciences technology company founded in 2020 with a singular mission: to build a foundational platform that connects the dynamic behavior of living cells to their underlying molecular identities — at the scale and resolution needed to transform drug discovery, cell therapy, and basic research.

For decades, biology has faced a fundamental trade-off: you can either watch what a cell does (live imaging), or read what it expresses (sequencing) — but not both, on the same cell. Cellanome's CellCage™ technology and the R3200 platform eliminate this trade-off by encapsulating individual live cells in semi-permeable hydrogel compartments, enabling longitudinal observation followed by whole-transcriptome sequencing of the exact same cells.

key_quote.text: Cellanome allows researchers to observe live cells in action, measure their functional responses, and then molecularly dissect their identities — creating a unified, high-dimensional dataset that was previously impossible to generate.

sections.title: Technology
sections.subtitle: The R3200 Platform & CellCage™ Technology
sections.content: The R3200 is Cellanome's flagship automated cell analysis instrument. It integrates fluorescent imaging, AI-powered cell segmentation, and micro-3D printing to build customized CellCage™ enclosures around individual cells or co-cultures — all within a flow cell that is subsequently used for RNA sequencing.

how_it_works.title: Encapsulation
how_it_works.description: Cells are loaded onto a flow cell. The R3200 uses photopolymerization — focused light — to form semi-permeable hydrogel walls (CellCages) around individual cells or co-cultures in real time. Each cage is sub-nanoliter in volume.

how_it_works.title: Live Observation
how_it_works.description: The semi-permeable cage allows media, nutrients, drugs, and reagents to flow freely in and out. Cells are imaged over time — tracking division, morphology, secretion, cytotoxicity, and cell-cell interactions.

how_it_works.title: Molecular Profiling
how_it_works.description: After functional observation, cells are lysed in situ. RNA is captured by spatial barcodes on the flow cell surface, enabling the transcriptome of each specific cell to be computationally linked back to its cage and imaging history.

key_differentiators.description: Retains cells & macromolecules; freely passes media, enzymes, primers
Light-triggered, spatially precise cage formation around selected cells
Automated identification and targeting of individual cells or clusters
Each cage position linked to unique DNA barcode for computational tracing
Sequential reagent exchange without material loss — enables complex assays
Integrated data analysis linking imaging phenotypes to transcriptomic data

sections.scientific_validation: The underlying capsule technology was independently validated in a landmark paper published in Science (Mazelis et al., Harvard Medical School, December 2025). The study demonstrated that semi-permeable capsules (CAGEs) enable high-throughput multistep assays combining live-cell culture with genome-wide readouts — increasing the number of colonies analyzed by ~1,000× over previous well-based methods.

sections.title: Team & Leadership
sections.subtitle: The Illumina Alumni Network
sections.content: Cellanome's leadership team is arguably one of the most experienced groups in the life sciences tools industry. The founding team and key executives are largely alumni of Illumina — the company that defined and dominated the DNA sequencing market for two decades. This is the same team that took sequencing from a $1,000 genome to a $100 genome.

sections.title: Funding
sections.subtitle: $213M+ Raised from Elite Investors
sections.content: Cellanome has raised over $213 million across three funding rounds. The $150M Series B in January 2024 — led by Premji Invest and DFJ Growth — was one of the largest private life sciences tools rounds of the year, reflecting strong conviction in the team and technology.

investors.name: Premji Invest
investors.description: Azim Premji's family office; long-term patient capital

investors.name: DFJ Growth
investors.description: Tier-1 growth equity; backed SpaceX, Tesla, Skype

investors.name: Wing Venture Capital
investors.description: Enterprise tech focus; deep science expertise

investors.name: 8VC
investors.description: Joe Lonsdale's firm; life sciences & deep tech

investors.name: SV Angel
investors.description: Ron Conway's firm; legendary early-stage investor

sections.title: Market Opportunity
sections.subtitle: A Rapidly Expanding Multi-Billion Dollar Market
sections.content: Cellanome operates at the intersection of two of the fastest-growing segments in life sciences tools: single-cell analysis and multi-omics. The single-cell analysis market was valued at ~$4.3B in 2024, growing at a CAGR of 10–15%. The broader multi-omics market is projected to reach $13.2B by 2035.

sections.title: Competitive Landscape
sections.subtitle: A Differentiated Position in a Crowded Space
sections.content: Cellanome operates in the single-cell analysis and live-cell biology space alongside several well-funded competitors. However, its unique combination of live-cell tracking, transcriptomics, and cell-cell interaction analysis creates a differentiated position that no single competitor currently replicates.

sections.title: Patents & IP
sections.subtitle: A Growing Patent Portfolio
sections.content: Cellanome has been aggressively building its intellectual property portfolio since its founding. The company has been granted 7 US patents and has filed multiple PCT applications, with key inventors including Tarun Kumar Khurana, Ali Agah, Yir-Shyuan Wu, and Filiz Gorpe Yasar.

patents.title: Devices and methods for analyzing biological samples (foundational)
Selective photopolymerization energy delivery for polymer matrix generation
Fluidic device analyte processing with optical energy source
Systems for screening biological components using hydrogel chambers
Detecting intercellular interactions of cells in compartments
Imaging and analyzing a cell in a compartment of a fluidic device
Incubating and analyzing a cell in a compartment of a fluidic device

sections.title: Scientific Publications
sections.subtitle: Peer-Reviewed Validation
sections.content: Cellanome's technology has been independently validated in top-tier scientific journals and presented at major conferences including AGBT, AACR, and JITC.

publications.title: Multi-step genomics on single cells and live cultures in sub-nanoliter capsules
publications.authors: Mazelis et al., Harvard Medical School
publications.key_finding: Landmark independent validation of semi-permeable capsule technology. ~1,000× more colonies analyzed than well-based methods.

publications.title: High-throughput single-cell omics using semipermeable capsules
publications.key_finding: Co-submitted companion paper demonstrating scRNA-seq of acute myeloid leukemia patient samples, uncovering distinct leukemic cell phenotypes.

publications.title: Establishing a multi-modal, cell therapy characterization workflow to identify cell subsets with heightened cytotoxicity
publications.key_finding: Demonstrated Cellanome's ability to identify cytotoxic T-cells (~85% accuracy) comparable to flow cytometry, while also capturing molecular identity.

publications.title: Leveraging a novel, multi-modal platform to identify drug resistant clones with distinct mechanisms of resistance to EGFR inhibitors
publications.key_finding: Used the R3200 platform to identify drug-resistant cancer cell clones and their molecular mechanisms of resistance — a key pharmaceutical use case.

publications.title: Unveiling genes in dendritic cells that enhance T cell priming through functional multiplexed single cell-cell interaction analysis
publications.key_finding: Demonstrated CellCage enclosures for co-culture of dendritic cells and T-cells to study immune cell interactions at single-cell resolution.

sections.title: Application Areas
sections.subtitle: Broad Applicability Across Biology
sections.content: The R3200 platform is designed as a horizontal tool — applicable across multiple research domains wherever understanding the link between cellular behavior and molecular identity is valuable.

areas.description: Identify cytotoxic T-cells by observing tumor-killing behavior, then sequence only the effective cells — dramatically improving CAR-T and TCR therapy development.
Observe how cancer cell clones respond to EGFR inhibitors over time, then sequence resistant clones to identify the molecular mechanisms of resistance.
Multi-modal characterization of cell therapy products — linking functional potency to transcriptomic identity for regulatory submissions.
Measure the persistence of gene expression programs across tens of thousands of expanding clones — 1000× more colonies than well-based methods.
Unveil genes in dendritic cells that enhance T-cell priming through functional multiplexed single cell-cell interaction analysis.
Long-term clonal expansion of human iPSCs and mouse hematopoietic stem cells within CellCage enclosures, followed by transcriptomic profiling.

sections.title: Commercialization
sections.subtitle: From Stealth to Commercial Launch
sections.content: After operating in stealth mode for four years, Cellanome began publicly showcasing its platform at scientific conferences in late 2024 and 2025. The company is now in active commercialization, with R3200 instruments installed at customer sites in the US and Europe.

timeline.event: Company founded by Mostafa Ronaghi and team; operating in stealth
$150M Series B raised; company begins limited public disclosure
Mostafa Ronaghi appears on Timmerman Report podcast; first public interview
CEO Omead Ostadan debuts on Mendelspod podcast; R3200 platform officially launched
Landmark Science paper validates capsule technology (Harvard Medical School)
Matthew Spitzer and Pier Federico Gherardini discuss using CellCage for immune cell research (Mendelspod)
First European R3200 installation at VIB Technologies, Ghent, Belgium
Cellanome showcases platform at AGBT 2026; multiple posters and presentations
AACR Annual Meeting — Poster #4287 in Immunology: CAR T Cell Functional Enhancement Session

risks.description: The R3200 is newly launched. Customer adoption, instrument reliability, and reagent consumable revenue are unproven at scale. Workflow integration into existing lab pipelines may be slower than expected.
10x Genomics has dominant market share, a large installed base, and is actively developing live-cell imaging integrations (Xenium). They have significant resources to compete.
The CellCage workflow is more complex than droplet-based methods. Researcher adoption requires training and workflow changes. Simplicity is a key selling point of competitors.
With $213M raised and ~105 employees, the company needs continued capital to scale manufacturing, sales, and support. Series C terms and timeline are unclear.
If Cellanome pursues clinical diagnostics applications, FDA regulatory pathways add significant time and cost. Current focus is research-use-only (RUO).
The company's credibility is closely tied to Mostafa Ronaghi and Jay Flatley. Their continued involvement is important for investor and customer confidence.

sections.title: Investment Thesis
sections.subtitle: Why Cellanome Could Be the Next Illumina
sections.content:
The bull case for Cellanome rests on a simple but powerful analogy: just as Illumina's sequencing platform became the indispensable tool for genomics research, Cellanome's R3200 has the potential to become the indispensable tool for the next generation of cell biology research — one that is increasingly focused on understanding cellular dynamics, not just static snapshots.

The convergence of AI-driven drug discovery, cell therapy development, and functional genomics creates a massive and growing demand for exactly the kind of data that Cellanome generates. The company has the right team (Illumina veterans), the right technology (independently validated in Science), the right IP (7 granted patents), and the right investors (Premji, DFJ, Wing) to execute on this vision.
sections.bottom_line: Cellanome represents a compelling investment opportunity in a company that is building foundational infrastructure for the next era of biology. The risks are real — commercialization is early, competition is fierce, and the technology is complex. But the team has done this before, the science is validated, and the market is enormous. For an investor with a 5–10 year horizon and appetite for life sciences tools, Cellanome deserves serious consideration.
